source_pr.title: planner/core: migrate test-infra to testify for `planner/core/plan_test.go`
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #28390

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test

Side effects
NA

Documentation
NA

### Release note

<!-- bugfix or new feature needs a release note -->

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: - c.Skip("skip race test") | tk.MustQuery("explain format='brief' select * from t force index(i) where id <= 1024 and c1 >= 0 and c1 <= 1024 and c2 in (2, 4, 6, 8) order by c1 limit 960").Check(testkit.Rows( (planner/core/plan_test.go)
failure_signature: - c.Skip("skip race test") | tk.MustQuery("explain format='brief' select * from t force index(i) where id <= 1024 and c1 >= 0 and c1 <= 1024 and c2 in (2, 4, 6, 8) order by c1 limit 960").Check(testkit.Rows( (planner/core/plan_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it asserted an exact plan/EXPLAIN output that can vary between runs. Exact plan/EXPLAIN assertions are brittle when optimizer choices can vary. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: - c.Skip("skip race test") | tk.MustQuery("explain format='brief' select * from t force index(i) where id <= 1024 and c1 >= 0 and c1 <= 1024 and c2 in (2, 4, 6, 8) order by c1 limit 960").Check(testkit.Rows( (planner/core/plan_test.go)
